zh.Pilkington Manor, Lancashire, England: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾皮爾金頓
zh.of Pilkington, Lancashire, England: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾皮爾金頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Roger de Pilkington II於從1291年1月8日到1292年1月7日出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾皮爾金頓時，他的父親Sir Roger de Pilkington Snr38歲，母親Amery de Barton38歲。他於大約1324年與Alicia de Bury結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1343年5月24日在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國藍卡爾皮爾金頓。

zh-hans.Pilkington Manor, Lancashire, England: 英国英格兰藍卡爾皮爾金頓
zh-hans.of Pilkington, Lancashire, England: 英国英格兰藍卡爾皮爾金頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Roger de Pilkington II于从1291年1月8日到1292年1月7日出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾皮爾金頓时，他的父亲Sir Roger de Pilkington Snr38岁，母亲Amery de Barton38岁。他于大约1324年与Alicia de Bury结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1343年5月24日在他的家乡，埋葬在英国藍卡爾皮爾金頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pilkington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northwestern): habitational name from a place in Lancashire. The placename may derive from an Old English personal name Pīleca or Pīloc + patronymic -ing + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The surname is established in Ireland, where its presence was first recorded in the early 15th century.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Roger de Pilkington II was born from 8 January 1291 to 7 January 1292, in Pilkington, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Roger de Pilkington Snr, was 38 and his mother, Amery de Barton, was 38. He married Alicia de Bury about 1324. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 24 May 1343, in his hometown, and was buried in Pilkington, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Roger de Pilkington II est né du 8 janvier 1291 au 7 janvier 1292, à Pilkington, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Roger de Pilkington Snr, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Amery de Barton, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Alicia de Bury environ 1324. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 24 mai 1343, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Pilkington, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Roger de Pilkington II von 8. Januar 1291 bis 7. Januar 1292 in Pilkington, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Roger de Pilkington Snr 38 und seine Mutter Amery de Barton 38. Er hat um 1324 Alicia de Bury geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 24. Mai 1343 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Pilkington, Lancashire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Roger de Pilkington II è nato il da 8 gennaio 1291 a 7 gennaio 1292 nel Pilkington, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Roger de Pilkington Snr, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Amery de Barton, aveva 38 anni. Lui sposato Alicia de Bury circa 1324. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 24 maggio 1343, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Pilkington, Lancashire, England.

ja.Pilkington, Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイアピルキントン

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Roger de Pilkington IIがから1291年1月8日まで1292年1月7日にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアピルキントンで生まれた時，父Sir Roger de Pilkington Snrは38歳，母Amery de Bartonは38歳であった。約1324年にAlicia de Buryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1343年5月24日に故郷で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアピルキントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Roger de Pilkington II(이)가 1291년 1월 8일부터 1292년 1월 7일까지 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 피킨그턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Roger de Pilkington Snr(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Amery de Barton(은)는 38세였다. 그는 대략 1324년 Alicia de Bury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1343년 5월 24일 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 피킨그턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Roger de Pilkington II nasceu de 8 de janeiro de 1291 a 7 de janeiro de 1292, em Pilkington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Roger de Pilkington Snr, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Amery de Barton, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Alicia de Bury aproximadamente 1324. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de maio de 1343, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Pilkington, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Roger de Pilkington II родился с 8 января 1291 по 7 января 1292 в местности пилкингтон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Roger de Pilkington Snr было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Amery de Barton, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Alicia de Bury около 1324 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 24 мая 1343 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности пилкингтон, ланкашир, Англия.
